content: I'm thinking about upgrading my laptop for video editing. My current one is so slow with 4K footage. Any recommendations?
For video editing, you'll want something with a powerful processor and good graphics. What's your budget range?
I was looking at MacBooks but they're so expensive. I've heard good things about the new Dell XPS 15. Have you worked with those?
The XPS 15 is excellent for video work - great color accuracy and processing power. Are you editing in 4K regularly?
Yes, I work on documentaries mostly. I'm based in Lisbon and just finished a project about local artisans. The 4K footage was beautiful but my old laptop struggled with rendering.

That sounds like an amazing project! For 4K editing, I'd definitely recommend at least 32GB RAM and a dedicated GPU. The XPS 15 would handle that well. Are you planning to stick with your current editing software?

I use Premiere Pro mostly. I'm also trying to learn DaVinci Resolve for color grading. My parents live with me and they keep asking when I'll be done with work so we can play chess - that's our evening ritual.
That's wonderful that you have that time with your parents. The XPS should give you better rendering speeds so you can finish projects faster. Have you considered external storage for your footage?
I have a couple of external SSDs but I'm running out of space. I might need to invest in a NAS system. Between work and taking care of my parents, I barely have time for my origami hobby anymore.
Maybe the new laptop will speed things up enough that I can get back to folding paper cranes in the evenings.
A NAS would be perfect for managing large video files. And I hope the upgrade gives you back some creative time - origami sounds like a great way to unwind after a long day of editing and caregiving.
